content_of_post_to_comment:
i’ve been trying to become more consistent with studying, but i keep falling into the same cycle.

i’ll have stuff i genuinely want to do and goals i care about, but when it’s actually time to start studying, especially after school or when i’m tired, i’ll end up scrolling instead. sometimes i’ll just pick up my phone for a “quick break” and then suddenly a ridiculous amount of time has passed.

the annoying thing is that i’m not lacking goals or motivation. i can get really motivated and have a super productive few days, but i don’t want my ability to study to depend on whether i happen to feel motivated that day. i want to actually build the habit of sitting down and starting, even when i’m tired or not in the mood.

for people who have managed to become genuinely consistent with studying or working, what actually helped? did you change your environment, use app blockers, create a specific routine, force yourself to start for a certain amount of time, or something else?

i’m not looking for “just be disciplined” or “delete your phone.” i’d really like to hear what actually worked for people over a long period of time, especially if you used to struggle with the same cycle of intending to work, getting distracted, and then feeling annoyed at yourself afterward.
